# Python binding for libpHash.
#
# Drives `setup.py build_ext --inplace` when a Python 3 interpreter and a
# working Cython installation are present. The binding itself recompiles
# src/pHash.cpp into the extension, so no separate pHash target dependency
# is needed -- but src/pHash.h must exist, which is generated by the
# top-level CONFIGURE_FILE step. We depend on that target ordering.

find_package(Python3 COMPONENTS Interpreter)
if(NOT Python3_FOUND)
    message(STATUS "Python 3 interpreter not found -- skipping Python bindings")
    return()
endif()

# Cython must be importable in the chosen interpreter.
execute_process(
    COMMAND ${Python3_EXECUTABLE} -c "import Cython"
    RESULT_VARIABLE _phash_cython_rc
    OUTPUT_QUIET ERROR_QUIET)
if(NOT _phash_cython_rc EQUAL 0)
    message(STATUS "Cython not importable in ${Python3_EXECUTABLE} -- skipping Python bindings")
    return()
endif()

add_custom_target(pHash-python ALL
    COMMAND ${Python3_EXECUTABLE} setup.py build_ext --inplace
    WORKING_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}
    COMMENT "Building pHash Python extension via setup.py"
    VERBATIM)

# The Python build #includes ../../src/pHash.h, which is generated by the
# top-level CONFIGURE_FILE. Ensure it's there first.
add_dependencies(pHash-python pHash)
